description: Content format patterns that drive virality: POV, story time, reaction, trend participation

rules.title: POV Format: Highest Relatability Hook
rules.description: POV (Point of View) format creates immediate first-person immersion. POV videos on TikTok get 2.8x more comments than third-person content. Hook line: 'POV: You [relatable situation]' grabs in 1.5s.

rules.title: Story Time: Open Loop Creates Must-Watch
rules.description: Story time format with an open loop hook ('I can't believe this happened to me…') achieves 71% average completion on TikTok. The unresolved tension forces watch-through. Best 30-60s duration.

rules.title: Reaction Format: Social Proof Through Response
rules.description: Reaction videos leverage existing viral content + creator commentary. Average watch time is 1.9x higher than original commentary content. The familiar anchor + new perspective formula triggers curiosity.

rules.title: Before/After: Highest Save Rate Format
rules.description: Before/after transformation content drives the highest save rate of any format (avg 8.4% save rate vs 2.1% baseline). Viewers save to reference later. Works for beauty, fitness, home, food, skills.

rules.title: Trend Participation: Speed Is Everything
rules.description: Participating in a trend within 24h of its peak gives 4.1x more reach than after 72h. After 5 days, trend content gets 89% less distribution. Template: use trend format + niche-specific twist for differentiation.

rules.title: Did You Know: Educational Hook Drives Shares
rules.description: 'Did you know [surprising fact]?' is the #1 shared hook format for educational content. Share rate 3.4x higher than standard teaching format. Fact must be counter-intuitive or challenge common belief.

rules.title: Number Lists Drive Completion: '5 things you didn't know'
rules.description: Numbered list format ('5 things', 'Top 3 reasons') achieves 64% completion rate by creating an expectation loop. Each number is a micro-hook. Odd numbers (3, 5, 7) outperform even numbers in engagement.

rules.title: Contrast/Conflict Hook Stops the Scroll
rules.description: Opening with contrast ('Everyone thinks X, but actually Y') stops scroll 2.2x better than agreement-based hooks. Conflict creates cognitive dissonance that the brain must resolve by continuing to watch.

recommendation.hook_template: Everyone thinks X, but actually Y
